title: AdvisorHookResult declares 8 top-level fields but only 2 survive into model-visible brief (~6 load-bearing sub-fields / ~26 total = 0.23 S/N)
Brief is double-rendered; codex adapter passes stricter thresholds into second render that can diverge from producer-side filter
Model-visible cost is 12-28 tokens; minimal schema = 5 top-level fields; maximal schema has no current consumer
Four per-runtime adapters diverge on 7 axes (input field fallbacks, cwd resolution, input source, output shape, timeout, threshold override, side-effects)
.claude/settings.local.json wires copilot adapter at top-level `bash:` key while nesting the correct claude adapter inside hooks[0].command — active config-shape bug
Gemini wires the user-prompt hook on BeforeAgent, not a UserPromptSubmit-equivalent event — different firing cadence and silent failure path if payload shape drifts
No .copilot/ directory or in-repo settings; copilot wiring is presumed user-machine-level and unverifiable from repo audit
Advisor library has zero runtime-specific branching except cache-key partitioning and codex-only thresholds — all runtime divergence is isolated to the four thin adapters (good separation)
Legacy parity test asserts all four adapters run; does NOT exercise the settings-driven invocation path so F23.1-bug would not be caught by CI

summary: Closed. 6/26 load-bearing sub-fields (~0.23 S/N). Brief is double-rendered with codex threshold divergence. Minimal schema = 5 fields, 12-28 tokens per prompt.

Closed. Four adapters diverge on 7 axes (F22); .claude/settings.local.json has active config-shape bug (F23.1); gemini uses BeforeAgent event (F23.2); copilot has no in-repo wiring (F23.3); library itself is runtime-agnostic (F24); legacy parity tests miss settings-path (F25).

title: Fix .claude/settings.local.json to drop the top-level bash:copilot entry; only the nested hooks[0].command:claude entry should remain. Repeats across UserPromptSubmit/PreCompact/SessionStart/Stop — all four blocks need the same fix.
Remove double-rendering in skill-advisor-brief.ts: either have adapters trust result.brief when non-null, or stop pre-rendering on the producer side.

Rename AdvisorHookResult to AdvisorHookInstrumentedResult; export a Minimal Brief type (5 fields from F21) and use it as the renderer's input contract. Clarifies which fields are telemetry vs model-visible.
Add a tests/current tier parity test that exercises the settings-driven invocation path (spawning each runtime's hook binary with its settings-specified wiring) so F23.1-class bugs become CI-visible.
